title: [CI:DOCS] Makefile: add warning about RHEL8 failing man pages
body:
The new man-page table check (#19278) fails on RHEL8 because
go-md2man is 2.00 and we require 2.02.

After much deliberation.... just ignore the problem. Add a
bold note to that part of the Makefile, and hope that when
the test fails on the next RHEL branching someone will look
at the Makefile, find the comment, and disable that test.

Got any better ideas?

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
If the RHEL 8 man pages are generated incorrectly should we not fix them?

The "fix" would make the man pages on all other distros less useful. It requires removing asterisks, underscores, backticks, and any other formatting from any table elements:
```diff
If for example _amount_ is **5** the second mapping step looks like:                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                
-|   intermediate UID   |    container UID    |                                                                                                                 
-| ------------------   | ----------------    |                                                                                                                 
-| *from_uid*           | *container_uid*     |                                                                                                                 
-| *from_uid* + 1       | *container_uid* + 1 |                                                                                                                 
-| *from_uid* + 2       | *container_uid* + 2 |                                                                                                                 
-| *from_uid* + 3       | *container_uid* + 3 |                                                                                                                 
-| *from_uid* + 4       | *container_uid* + 4 |                                                                                                                 
+| intermediate UID | container UID    |                                                                                                                        
+|------------------|------------------|                                                                                                                        
+| fromUID          | containerUID     |                                                                                                                        
+| fromUID + 1      | containerUID + 1 |                                                                                                                        
+| fromUID + 2      | containerUID + 2 |                                                                                                                        
+| fromUID + 3      | containerUID + 3 |                                                                                                                        
+| fromUID + 4      | containerUID + 4 |
```
(that's just one example among many. I gave up even trying).

Since this is 100% a bug in go-md2man, and it is fixed in Fedora and RHEL9, I would prefer not to deformat our man pages just for RHEL8.

well I mean the simple answer would be to ask RHEL 8 to update the go-md2man package, did we already do this?

If users end up with unreadable man pages this is bad and I don't think we should ignore it, sooner or later a customer will file a bug about that.

Actually..... there's an even easier solution:
```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index f931e0f89..5444f9f66 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -148 +148 @@ export PATH := $(shell $(GO) env GOPATH)/bin:$(PATH)
-GOMD2MAN ?= $(shell command -v go-md2man || echo './test/tools/build/go-md2man')
+GOMD2MAN ?= './test/tools/build/go-md2man'       <---- this is already 2.02
```
Can anyone tell me why we have that `command` thing? Why would we possibly want to use the system `go-md2man` instead of the solid, reliable, guaranteed, vendored one?
I have no idea other then tradition, I think we should use the internal one, and no longer rely on the system one.
